metadata.description: Country pet import/export regulations database: all 10 P1 countries
metadata.methodology: Data compiled from official government portals (Block 3 raw data), airline policy research, route keyword research cross-references, and published regulatory frameworks. UAE, Canada, Hong Kong, and South Africa government portals blocked automated extraction; data compiled from published guidelines, embassy documentation, and cross-referenced with multiple secondary sources.
metadata.batch_1: Block 5: UK, USA, UAE, Australia, Canada
metadata.batch_2: Block 6: Germany, France, Singapore, Hong Kong, South Africa

UK.notes: Post-Brexit, Great Britain (England, Scotland, Wales) has separate rules from Northern Ireland. GB-issued pet passports no longer valid for EU entry. This record covers Great Britain unless noted otherwise.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted BEFORE rabies vaccination. Vaccine is invalid if microchipped after.
microchip.notes: If pet has non-ISO chip, owner must bring own scanner. Strongly recommended to use ISO standard.

rabies_vaccination.booster_condition: No wait required after booster only if no break in vaccination cover
rabies_vaccination.vaccine_type: Inactivated virus or recombinant. Must be WHO-approved.
rabies_vaccination.notes: 21 full days from date of first vaccination (or last in initial course) before travel

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from unlisted countries (countries not on UK Part 1 or Part 2 lists)
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from listed countries (all P1 countries are listed)
rabies_titre_test.test_type: FAVN or ELISA at APHA-approved lab
rabies_titre_test.notes: Blood drawn at least 30 days after rabies vaccination. If from unlisted country, 3-month wait from successful test before travel.

quarantine.penalty_trigger: Pet arrives without correct documentation, wrong route, or non-compliant vaccination/microchip
quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for pets arriving with correct documentation from any country. Penalty quarantine only.

import_permit.alternative: Pet travel document (AHC or pet passport) serves as permit equivalent
import_permit.notes: No formal import permit like Australia. The travel document system replaces it.

health_certificate.name: Animal Health Certificate (AHC) or EU Pet Passport
health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian in country of departure
health_certificate.notes: From EU countries: EU pet passport OR AHC accepted. From non-EU countries: AHC or equivalent. GB-issued pet passports NOT valid for EU entry (post-Brexit).

tapeworm_treatment.timing: 24-120 hours before scheduled arrival time in GB
tapeworm_treatment.drug: Praziquantel or equivalent (must contain active ingredient effective against Echinococcus multilocularis)
tapeworm_treatment.administered_by: Veterinarian (must be recorded in pet travel document)
tapeworm_treatment.notes: Very strict timing window. Treatment outside 24-120 hour window = non-compliant.

banned_breeds.notes: Cannot bring banned breed into GB unless valid Certificate of Exemption already held. Assessment is type-based not breed-based (dog that 'substantially conforms' to type characteristics).
banned_breeds.exemption: Existing exempted dogs can travel if Certificate of Exemption is valid

approved_routes.description: Must use an approved travel route for pet entry. Approved routes listed on GOV.UK.
approved_routes.notes: Not all routes/carriers are approved. Check GOV.UK approved route list before booking.

approved_entry_ports: Various approved ferry ports and Eurotunnel terminal

maximum_pets.exception: More than 5 if attending competition, show, or sporting event (with proof)

additional_requirements: Non-commercial movement declaration required
Pet must travel with owner or within 5 days of owner's travel
Pet must not be sold or transferred within GB

to_eu.documents: Animal Health Certificate from APHA-authorised vet (GB pet passport NOT accepted post-Brexit)
to_eu.tapeworm: Check destination country requirements (some EU states require it for dogs)
to_eu.point_of_entry: Must enter EU through travellers' point of entry
to_eu.notes: Post-Brexit, UK to EU pet travel requires AHC instead of pet passport. Book AHC appointment with vet well in advance.

to_non_eu.documents: Export health certificate from APHA-authorised vet, endorsed if required by destination
to_non_eu.notes: Requirements vary by destination country. Contact destination country's authority for specific import requirements.

export_permit.notes: No formal export permit for pets. AHC or export health certificate serves as documentation.

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Rabies-free/low-risk (only CDC Dog Import Form needed)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 3 (approved, rabies-free, titre test required, 10-day quarantine)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard AHC entry, no titre test)

USA.notes: Federal requirements differ significantly between dogs and cats. CDC manages dog imports. USDA APHIS manages export endorsements. Individual US states may have additional requirements.

dogs.governing_body: CDC (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention)

classification_system.high_risk_list: CDC maintains list of 100+ countries with high risk of dog rabies

microchip.timing: Must be implanted BEFORE rabies vaccination (or vaccine is invalid)

cdc_dog_import_form.notes: Valid for multiple entries if dog stays in same risk-category country

requirements_low_risk_origin.rabies_vaccination: Strongly recommended but NOT required by CDC
requirements_low_risk_origin.arrival: Can arrive at any airport, seaport, or land border crossing

requirements_high_risk_us_vaccinated.description: Dogs with valid US-issued rabies vaccination returning from high-risk country
documents: Certification of U.S.-issued Rabies Vaccination form (completed by USDA-accredited vet, endorsed by USDA)
requirements_high_risk_us_vaccinated.vaccination_age: At least 12 weeks old at vaccination
requirements_high_risk_us_vaccinated.arrival: Can arrive at any airport, seaport, or land border crossing
requirements_high_risk_us_vaccinated.form_note: USDA-endorsed export health certificates issued after July 31, 2025 no longer accepted for re-entry to US

requirements_high_risk_foreign_vaccinate.description: Dogs from high-risk countries with foreign-issued rabies vaccination
documents: Certification of Foreign Rabies Vaccination and Microchip form (vet-completed, endorsed by government vet in country of departure)
Reservation confirmation at CDC-registered animal care facility
Valid rabies serology titer report from CDC-approved lab (OR agree to 28-day quarantine)
requirements_high_risk_foreign_vaccinate.vaccination_age: At least 12 weeks old at first vaccination

serology_titre.blood_draw_timing: At least 30 days after first valid rabies vaccine AND at least 28 days before US entry
serology_titre.validity: Valid for life if no lapse in rabies vaccination coverage
serology_titre.alternative: 28-day quarantine at CDC-registered facility if no valid titre

arrival.restriction: MUST arrive by air at airport where CDC-registered animal care facility is located
arrival.notes: Cannot fly to a different US city first. No domestic travel until after facility services.

requirements_high_risk_foreign_vaccinate.form_validity: 30 days after vet signature, single use only

timeline.60_days_before: Microchip, verify rabies vaccination, begin airline booking, collect blood for serology
timeline.30_days_before: Last chance for serology blood draw. Reserve spot at CDC facility. Get Certification form completed.
timeline.week_of_travel: Finalize travel. Complete CDC Dog Import Form online.
timeline.day_of_travel: Arrive at correct airport. Present all documents.

cats.federal_requirements: NONE. APHIS Veterinary Services has NO animal health requirements for importing pet cats into the United States from a foreign country.
cats.cdc_requirements: Check CDC website for any applicable requirements (currently minimal for cats)
cats.fish_wildlife: Check US Fish and Wildlife Service for CITES species
cats.state_requirements: Individual US states may have their own import requirements. Check destination state agriculture department.
cats.notes: This is a massive differentiator. Cats face virtually no federal import barriers to the USA. Route pages should highlight this contrast with dog requirements.

microchip.timing: MUST be implanted BEFORE rabies vaccination or vaccine is considered invalid
microchip.notes: This is a critical requirement that many pet owners miss

quarantine.required_for: Dogs from high-risk countries without valid serology titre
quarantine.not_required_for: Dogs from low-risk countries, dogs with valid titre, all cats
quarantine.notes: Quarantine may be shortened if dog receives US-licensed rabies vaccine and meets titre requirements during quarantine

import_permit.notes: No formal import permit. CDC Dog Import Form is the registration mechanism.

health_certificate.required_for_dogs: Certification forms serve this function (US-issued or Foreign Vaccination forms)
health_certificate.notes: No standalone health certificate requirement. The CDC certification forms include health attestation.

banned_breeds.state_local: Some states, counties, and cities have breed-specific legislation (BSL)
banned_breeds.notes: BSL varies widely. Check destination city/county. Some states (e.g. Florida) have banned BSL at state level.

approved_entry_ports.low_risk: Any airport, seaport, or land border crossing
approved_entry_ports.high_risk_without_titre: Only airports with CDC-registered animal care facility
approved_entry_ports.cdc_registered_facilities: Contact CDC for current list of registered facilities

additional_requirements: Dogs must be at least 6 months old at time of entry
Dogs must appear healthy upon arrival
All dogs need CDC Dog Import Form receipt

usda_endorsement.description: Most countries require USDA-endorsed health certificates for pets exported from the US
process: 1. Visit USDA-accredited veterinarian for health examination
2. Vet completes health certificate (country-specific format if required by destination)
3. Submit to USDA APHIS Veterinary Services for endorsement
usda_endorsement.timing: Health examination typically within 10 days of travel (varies by destination country requirements)
usda_endorsement.processing_time: 2-10 business days depending on method (drop-off, mail, or electronic)
usda_endorsement.electronic_option: VEHCS (Veterinary Export Health Certification System) available for some destinations
usda_endorsement.notes: USDA-accredited vet required. Not all vets are accredited. Find accredited vets at USDA APHIS website.

export_permit.notes: No formal export permit. USDA endorsement of health certificate serves as export documentation.

classification_by_other_countries.uk_apha: Listed country (standard AHC entry, no titre test for UK entry)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 4 (titre test required, 10+ day quarantine, import permit, 6-month prep)
classification_by_other_countries.singapore_avs: Category B (30-day home quarantine, titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.canada_cfia: Low-risk (rabies cert required for dogs 3+ months)

UAE.notes: UAE pet import regulations are managed at both federal (MOCCAE) and emirate (municipality) level. Dubai and Abu Dhabi have the most established processes. Government portals blocked automated extraction; data compiled from MOCCAE published guidelines, embassy documentation, and established pet transport operator information. Verify all requirements with the relevant municipality before travel.
UAE.data_confidence_note: UAE government portals were inaccessible during automated research. Data cross-referenced against multiple secondary sources and airline requirements. Re-verify against official MOCCAE/municipality sources before publishing.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Standard ISO microchip required. Non-ISO may not be accepted.

rabies_vaccination.validity_period: Must be within validity period stated by manufacturer (typically 1-3 years)
rabies_vaccination.notes: Vaccination must be administered after microchipping. Titre test may be required from certain countries.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from certain non-approved or high-risk countries
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from approved countries (UK, USA, Canada, Australia, EU countries, etc.)
rabies_titre_test.notes: Check current MOCCAE requirements as this may vary by emirate

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for pets from approved countries with correct documentation. Pets with incomplete documentation may be quarantined at owner's expense.

dubai.application: Online via Dubai Municipality website or app

abu_dhabi.authority: Abu Dhabi Agriculture and Food Safety Authority (ADAFSA)

import_permit.notes: Import permit MUST be obtained before travel. Different emirates have different application processes. Allow 1-2 weeks for processing.

health_certificate.name: Veterinary health certificate / Official health certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Government veterinarian or government-endorsed veterinarian in country of export
health_certificate.government_endorsement: Must be endorsed by official veterinary authority of exporting country (e.g. USDA for US, APHA for UK, DAFF for Australia)
health_certificate.notes: Certificate must confirm: rabies vaccination status, microchip number, pet is free from infectious disease, fit to travel

other_vaccinations.timing: Must be current and within manufacturer's stated validity period

banned_breeds.legislation: Federal Law and individual emirate regulations
restricted_breeds: Staffordshire Bull Terrier (restricted in some emirates)
Rottweiler (restricted but not outright banned in most emirates)
banned_breeds.notes: Breed restrictions vary by emirate. Some breeds require special permits. Wolf hybrids banned. Check specific emirate regulations. List may not be exhaustive.

maximum_pets.notes: Check with relevant municipality for limits per household

temperature_restrictions.reason: Ground temperatures at airports can exceed 45C. Airlines impose heat embargoes.
temperature_restrictions.airlines_affected: Emirates SkyCargo, Etihad Cargo, and others suspend or restrict pet cargo during summer months
temperature_restrictions.notes: Plan travel outside summer months. Some airlines may still transport in temperature-controlled facilities but availability is limited.

additional_requirements: Internal and external parasite treatment recommended
Blood test for Leishmania may be required from endemic countries
NOC (No Objection Certificate) from building/community if renting (not a government requirement but practical consideration)

documents_needed: Original pet passport or vaccination record
Veterinary health certificate (issued within 48 hours of travel)
Import permit from destination country (if applicable)
export_permit.notes: Contact municipality for specific export clearance process

government_endorsement.authority: MOCCAE (Ministry of Climate Change and Environment) or emirate municipality
government_endorsement.notes: Health certificate must be endorsed for export

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: HIGH-RISK for dog rabies. Foreign-vaccinated dog process applies. CDC-registered facility required. 28-day quarantine possible.
classification_by_other_countries.uk_apha: Listed country (standard AHC entry, no titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: May not be approved for direct import. Check current DAFF classification. Pet may need to reside in approved country first.
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)

Australia.notes: Among the strictest pet import regimes globally. All imported cats and dogs undergo mandatory quarantine. Only pets from approved countries (Groups 1-6) can be imported. Allow minimum 6 months to complete all import steps. DAFF strongly recommends using an experienced pet transport agent.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before or on same day as rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Microchip must be readable at all stages of the import process

rabies_vaccination.protocol: Two vaccinations required, not less than 30 days apart, not more than 365 days apart
rabies_vaccination.notes: Vaccination schedule must be completed before titre test blood draw

rabies_titre_test.test_type: RNATT (Rabies Neutralising Antibody Titre Test) / FAVN
rabies_titre_test.blood_draw_timing: At least 30 days after second rabies vaccination

wait_after_test.group_3: No additional wait after successful result (beyond standard processing)
wait_after_test.group_4: 180 days from blood draw date (not result date)

rabies_titre_test.validity: Valid for life if no lapse in rabies vaccination cover and annual boosters maintained
rabies_titre_test.notes: The 180-day wait for Group 4+ is the biggest timeline driver. Blood must be drawn at least 30 days after second vaccination, then 180-day wait from draw date. This is why 6-month lead time is needed.

quarantine.applies_to: ALL imported cats and dogs regardless of origin
quarantine.typical_duration_days: 10-30 depending on country group and documentation

facility.name: Mickleham Post Entry Quarantine Facility
facility.booking: Must be pre-booked. Limited spaces. Book early.

quarantine.what_happens: Documentation assessed, animal health monitored for diseases of biosecurity concern

heat_stress_warning.at_risk: Brachycephalic breeds, long-haired, overweight, elderly, or medically compromised animals
heat_stress_warning.notes: DAFF issues explicit heat stress warnings for summer arrivals

operational.veterinary_care: If needed, importer arranges private vet at own expense. Biosecurity officers may transport to emergency vet hospital.

import_permit.issuing_authority: Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF)
import_permit.application: Online through BICON (Biosecurity Import Conditions) system
import_permit.notes: Must be obtained BEFORE travel. Permit specifies conditions for import.

health_certificate.name: Government-issued veterinary health certificate (country-specific format)
health_certificate.issued_by: Government veterinarian in country of export
health_certificate.format: DAFF negotiates specific certificate format with each approved country. If no certificate has been negotiated with a country, pets cannot be exported from there.
health_certificate.endorsement: Must be endorsed by official veterinary authority of exporting country
health_certificate.notes: Certificate format is NON-NEGOTIABLE. Must use exact DAFF-approved format for the specific country of export.

country_grouping_system.description: Australia classifies approved countries into groups 1-6 with escalating requirements

group_1.name: Rabies-free countries with similar health status
group_1.requirements: Simplest process. No rabies vaccination needed.

group_2.name: Approved rabies-free countries/territories
group_2.requirements: No rabies vaccination needed. Standard health checks.
group_2.notes: Some do not currently have negotiated veterinary health certificates

group_3.name: Approved countries requiring rabies testing
group_3.requirements: Rabies vaccination + titre test. No 180-day wait.

group_4.name: Approved countries with higher rabies risk
group_4.requirements: Rabies vaccination + titre test + 180-day wait from blood draw.
group_4.notes: The 180-day wait is the critical constraint for these countries.

group_5.name: Approved countries with significant rabies risk

non_approved.description: Countries not in any approved group cannot export directly to Australia
non_approved.workaround: Pet must reside in an approved country long enough to complete all import steps from that country
non_approved.uae_status: UAE may not be in an approved group. Verify current DAFF classification. If not approved, pet must transit through approved country (e.g. UK).

banned_breeds.federal: No federal breed ban for imports
banned_breeds.state_level: Some Australian states have breed-specific legislation
banned_breeds.notes: Even if imported, restricted breeds may face state-level restrictions on ownership.

approved_entry_ports.primary: Melbourne Airport (MEL) - only port with quarantine facility
approved_entry_ports.notes: All pets MUST arrive via Melbourne and go to Mickleham. No other Australian airport accepts pet imports.

species_not_accepted: Most other pet species have separate requirements
additional_requirements: Internal and external parasite treatment within 5 days of export
Dogs: must test negative for Ehrlichia canis (tick disease)
Dogs: must test negative for Brucella canis
Dogs: Leptospirosis vaccination may be required
Cats: no additional tests beyond standard (simpler than dogs)
Blood tests for various diseases depending on country group
Veterinary treatments must be administered by registered veterinarian

export_permit.notes: Required for live animal exports from Australia

veterinary_inspection.timing: Within 5 days of export (or as specified by destination country)

government_endorsement.notes: Health certificate must be endorsed by DAFF for export

export_requirements.notes: Australia being rabies-free means Australian pets have simplified entry into many countries. The export process is the main requirement.

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Rabies-free/low-risk (only CDC Dog Import Form needed)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)

Canada.notes: Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) manages pet imports. Requirements are relatively straightforward compared to USA, Australia, or Singapore. Government portal blocked automated extraction; data compiled from published CFIA guidelines and cross-referenced with CBSA (Canada Border Services Agency) documentation. Important: Canada suspended import of commercial dogs from high-risk rabies countries in September 2022; personal pet imports may still be affected.
Canada.data_confidence_note: CFIA portal inaccessible during automated extraction. Data compiled from published guidelines and secondary sources. Verify with CFIA before publishing, particularly regarding the 2022 commercial dog import suspension and any subsequent updates.

microchip.notes: While not federally mandated in the same way as other countries, permanent identification (microchip or tattoo) is strongly recommended and required for many provincial regulations.

dogs.content: Certificate must identify animal (description, microchip/tattoo number), state vaccine product, date of vaccination, vaccination validity period, and be signed by licensed veterinarian
dogs.notes: Puppies under 3 months without rabies vaccination may have different requirements depending on whether they're from a high-risk country

cats.notes: Rabies vaccination not required by CFIA for cat imports. Individual provinces may require it.

rabies_titre_test.notes: Not required for standard pet imports from most countries

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for pet dogs or cats. Animals must appear healthy upon arrival.

import_permit.notes: Personal pet imports (1-2 animals) do not require an import permit. Commercial imports (for sale, breeding, adoption, fostering, or more than 2 dogs) require CFIA import permit.

personal_pets.required: Varies. CFIA does not require a health certificate for personal pet dogs with valid rabies certificate. However, airline may require one, and it's recommended.
personal_pets.notes: Practical recommendation: always obtain veterinary health certificate for air travel regardless of CFIA requirements.

commercial_imports.issued_by: Government veterinarian in country of export
commercial_imports.endorsement: Government endorsement of exporting country required

commercial_dog_import_suspension.description: Canada suspended import of commercial dogs from countries at high risk for dog rabies
commercial_dog_import_suspension.applies_to: Commercial imports (for sale, breeding, adoption, fostering, scientific research, exhibition)
commercial_dog_import_suspension.does_not_apply_to: Personal pets accompanying owner (with fewer than 2 dogs per person from high-risk countries)
commercial_dog_import_suspension.high_risk_countries: Over 100 countries classified as high-risk for dog rabies by CFIA (similar to CDC list)
commercial_dog_import_suspension.notes: This suspension mirrors CDC approach. Personal pet imports from high-risk countries still permitted with conditions.

banned_breeds.federal: No federal breed-specific legislation for imports
banned_breeds.provincial_municipal: Various provinces and municipalities have BSL
banned_breeds.ontario: Pit Bull ban (Dog Owners' Liability Act). Pit Bulls and related types banned since 2005.
banned_breeds.notes: Breed restrictions vary significantly by province and municipality. Check destination before travel.

approved_entry_ports.description: Pets can arrive at any Canadian international airport, seaport, or land border crossing
approved_entry_ports.land_borders: Dogs with valid rabies certificates can cross at any border point

species_accepted: Other pet species with specific requirements

other_vaccinations.dogs: Not required federally, but recommended (distemper, parvovirus, hepatitis)

additional_requirements: Dogs must appear healthy upon inspection
CBSA officer may inspect pet at border
Owner may be asked to present vaccination records
Young puppies (under 3 months, unvaccinated): may require signed declaration from owner
Provincial requirements may apply in addition to federal (check destination province)

provincial_variations.note: Provincial requirements vary and may be more restrictive than federal requirements
examples: Alberta: may require health certificate for dogs from other provinces
British Columbia: generally follows federal requirements

export_permit.notes: No CFIA export permit required for personal pet exports. Some destination countries may require CFIA endorsement of health certificate.

health_certificate.issued_by: CFIA District Veterinarian or accredited veterinarian (endorsed by CFIA if needed)
health_certificate.process: Visit accredited vet for examination, vet prepares health certificate, submit to CFIA for endorsement if destination requires government endorsement
health_certificate.timing: Typically within 10 days of travel (or as specified by destination country)

cfia_endorsement.required_for: Countries that require official government endorsement of health certificate

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Rabies-free/low-risk (only CDC Dog Import Form needed, land border option)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 4 (titre test, 180-day wait, 10+ day quarantine, import permit, 6-month prep)
classification_by_other_countries.singapore_avs: Category B (30-day home quarantine, titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)

Germany.notes: Germany follows EU harmonised pet movement rules (Regulation 576/2013, transitioning to new rules under Regulation 2026/131 from 22 April 2026). Federal Ministry of Food and Agriculture (BMEL) oversees. Individual Bundeslaender may have additional animal welfare rules. Germany is considered pet-friendly with high dog ownership rates.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before or at same time as rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Standard EU requirement. Must be readable by standard scanner.

rabies_vaccination.booster_condition: No wait if booster administered within validity period of previous vaccination
rabies_vaccination.notes: Standard EU 21-day wait from date of first vaccination before travel. No additional Germany-specific requirements beyond EU framework.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from unlisted third countries (countries not on EU Annex II list)
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from EU member states, listed third countries (all P1 countries are listed)
rabies_titre_test.test_type: FAVN or ELISA at EU-approved laboratory
rabies_titre_test.notes: Blood drawn at least 30 days after rabies vaccination. 3-month wait from successful test for unlisted countries. All P1 countries are listed, so titre test not required for P1 route imports.

quarantine.notes: No quarantine for pets entering with correct EU documentation. Non-compliant animals may be returned, quarantined at owner's expense, or in worst case euthanised.

import_permit.notes: No formal import permit. EU pet passport (intra-EU) or Animal Health Certificate (from third countries) serves as documentation.

from_eu.notes: Valid EU pet passport with current rabies vaccination and microchip records

from_non_eu_listed.issued_by: Official veterinarian in country of departure
from_non_eu_listed.notes: Must be endorsed by official veterinary authority of exporting country

from_non_eu_unlisted.document: Animal Health Certificate (AHC) plus rabies titre test results
from_non_eu_unlisted.notes: Additional titre test documentation needed for unlisted countries

tapeworm_treatment.notes: Germany does not require Echinococcus multilocularis tapeworm treatment for dogs entering the country. Some neighbouring EU states do (Finland, Ireland, Malta, Norway).

banned_breeds.import_ban_details: Import and transit of these breeds (and their crosses) into Germany is PROHIBITED. Exceptions possible with specific Bundesland approval and proof of non-dangerousness.
banned_breeds.bundesland_variations: Individual German states (Bundeslaender) have their own dangerous dog lists that may add breeds beyond the federal list. Bayern, Hessen, Brandenburg, Hamburg and others have additional breed restrictions.
banned_breeds.notes: Stricter than UK. Four breeds banned at federal level for IMPORT. This is a critical consideration for route pages involving Germany as destination.

approved_entry_ports.description: Pets can enter at designated travellers' points of entry
approved_entry_ports.notes: Must enter through designated travellers' point of entry with customs declaration

maximum_pets.exception: More than 5 if attending competition, show, or sporting event (with proof)

additional_requirements: Non-commercial declaration that pet is not being sold or transferred
Pet must travel with owner or within 5 days of owner's travel
Puppies under 15 weeks cannot enter (must be 12 weeks + 21-day vaccination wait = 15 weeks minimum)

to_eu.documents: EU Pet Passport (valid, with current rabies and microchip)
to_eu.notes: Free movement within EU with valid pet passport. No additional export documentation needed.

to_non_eu.documents: Export health certificate, endorsed by German veterinary authority if required by destination
to_non_eu.process: Visit authorised vet, obtain examination and certificate, submit for endorsement if needed
to_non_eu.authority: Landesamt or Kreisveterinäramt (state or district veterinary office)
to_non_eu.notes: Requirements vary by destination country. Contact destination country authority for specific import requirements.

export_permit.notes: No formal export permit for pets. Health certificate and EU pet passport serve as documentation.

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Rabies-free/low-risk (only CDC Dog Import Form needed)
classification_by_other_countries.uk_apha: Listed country (standard AHC entry, no titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 4 (titre test, 180-day wait, 10+ day quarantine, import permit)
classification_by_other_countries.singapore_avs: Category B (30-day home quarantine, titre test)

France.notes: France follows EU harmonised pet movement rules. Direction Generale de l'Alimentation (DGAL) under the Ministry of Agriculture oversees implementation. France is one of the most pet-friendly countries in Europe with high pet ownership, though some rental properties restrict pets. France has its own breed-specific legislation (Categories 1 and 2) that is notably strict.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before or at same time as rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Tattoo identification accepted only if clearly legible AND applied before 3 July 2011. Otherwise microchip required.

rabies_vaccination.booster_condition: No wait if booster within validity period
rabies_vaccination.notes: Standard EU protocol. 21-day wait from first vaccination.

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from EU and listed third countries (all P1 countries listed)
rabies_titre_test.test_type: FAVN or ELISA at EU-approved laboratory
rabies_titre_test.notes: Same as Germany/EU standard. Not required for any P1 country routes.

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine. Non-compliant animals may be quarantined or returned at owner's expense.

import_permit.notes: No formal import permit. EU pet passport or AHC serves as documentation.

from_non_eu_listed.issued_by: Official veterinarian in country of departure

tapeworm_treatment.notes: France does not require Echinococcus tapeworm treatment for entering dogs. Treatment recommended but not mandatory.

banned_breeds.legislation: Law 99-5 of 6 January 1999, amended by Law 2008-582

category_1_banned.name: Attack dogs
breeds: Pit Bull type (not registered in studbook)
category_1_banned.restrictions: BANNED from import into France. Cannot be acquired, sold, given away, or imported. Must be sterilised. Must be muzzled and leashed in public. Cannot access public transport or public areas.
category_1_banned.import_note: Category 1 dogs CANNOT be imported into France under any circumstances.

category_2_restricted.name: Guard and defence dogs
category_2_restricted.restrictions: Restricted but not banned. Owner must: obtain a permit (permis de détention), have civil liability insurance, ensure dog is sterilised if not registered in studbook, muzzle and leash in public areas.
category_2_restricted.import_note: Category 2 dogs CAN be imported but owner must obtain permit and meet all conditions. Process should begin before arrival.

banned_breeds.notes: France has one of the strictest breed-specific frameworks in Europe. Category 1 is an outright import ban. Category 2 is heavily regulated. This is a critical content differentiator for France-destination route pages.

approved_entry_ports.ferry_ports: Multiple approved ferry ports for pet travel from UK

maximum_pets.exception: More than 5 if attending competition, show, or sporting event

additional_requirements: Non-commercial declaration
Pet must accompany owner or travel within 5 days
Puppies under 15 weeks cannot enter
Category 2 dogs require permis de détention and civil liability insurance

to_eu.notes: Free movement within EU with valid passport

to_non_eu.documents: Export health certificate endorsed by DDPP (Direction Départementale de la Protection des Populations)
to_non_eu.process: Vet examination, certificate completion, DDPP endorsement

export_permit.notes: No formal export permit. Health certificate and EU pet passport serve as documentation.

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Rabies-free/low-risk (only CDC Dog Import Form needed)
classification_by_other_countries.uk_apha: Listed country (standard AHC entry, no titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 4 (titre test, 180-day wait, 10+ day quarantine, import permit)
classification_by_other_countries.singapore_avs: Category B (30-day home quarantine, titre test)

Singapore.notes: National Parks Board (NParks) Animal and Veterinary Service (AVS) regulates all animal imports. Singapore maintains rabies-free status through a rigorous 4-tier category system. AVS website blocked automated extraction; data compiled from AVS published guidelines and official category system documentation. Singapore has strict housing rules: HDB flats (public housing, ~80% of population) only allow approved small dog breeds and cats (since 2024).

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Standard ISO microchip required by AVS

category_a.notes: Not required for imports from Category A rabies-free countries (Australia, UK, NZ, etc.)

category_b_c_d.timing: At least 30 days before export, within 12 months of export
category_b_c_d.notes: Vaccination must be administered after microchipping

rabies_titre_test.validity: Valid if rabies vaccination maintained without lapse
rabies_titre_test.notes: Must be conducted at an OIE reference laboratory or approved equivalent

category_a.notes: No quarantine for Category A imports (Australia, UK, NZ, Japan, etc.)

category_b.conditions: Pet confined to owner's residence for 30 days. No contact with other animals. AVS may inspect.
category_b.notes: USA, Canada, Germany, France, most EU countries fall into Category B

category_c.facility: AVS-appointed quarantine facility (Sembawang Animal Quarantine Station)
category_c.notes: UAE, Hong Kong, South Africa fall into Category C

category_d.notes: Highest risk countries. May need to reside in Cat A/B country first.

import_licence.name: AVS Import Licence
import_licence.application_system: CITES e-Licence System (formerly Online Licensing Management System)
import_licence.notes: Must be approved BEFORE pet arrives in Singapore. Apply online through NParks portal.

health_certificate.name: Veterinary health certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Government veterinarian or government-endorsed veterinarian in country of export
health_certificate.endorsement: Must be endorsed by official veterinary authority of exporting country
health_certificate.notes: Certificate must state: species, breed, age, sex, microchip number, vaccination records, health status

other_vaccinations.timing: Must be current and within manufacturer's validity period

parasite_treatment.internal: Deworming treatment within 7 days of export
parasite_treatment.external: Anti-tick and anti-flea treatment within 7 days of export
parasite_treatment.notes: Treatment must be recorded on health certificate

banned_breeds.legislation: Animals and Birds (Dog Licensing and Control) Rules
restricted_breeds: Pit Bull Terrier (including American Pit Bull Terrier, American Staffordshire Terrier, Staffordshire Bull Terrier)
banned_breeds.status: These breeds may not be imported into Singapore. Existing dogs of these breeds may be kept under strict conditions (sterilisation, muzzle, leash, insurance).
banned_breeds.hdb_approved_breeds: HDB (public housing) allows only approved small dog breeds. List maintained by HDB. Large dogs restricted to private properties.
banned_breeds.notes: More breeds restricted than most countries. Akita and Neapolitan Mastiff restrictions are unusual. HDB approval adds another layer for most Singapore residents.

maximum_pets.notes: Check AVS licence conditions for limits. HDB households limited to 1 dog (approved breeds) or up to 2 cats.

category_a.name: Category A: Approved rabies-free countries
category_a.summary: No quarantine, no rabies vaccination required, simplest process

category_b.name: Category B: Approved countries with controlled rabies
category_b.summary: 30-day home quarantine, rabies vaccination + titre test, endorsed health certificate

category_c.name: Category C: Countries requiring government quarantine
category_c.summary: 30-day government quarantine facility, rabies vaccination + titre test, import licence

category_d.name: Category D: Non-approved / highest risk
category_d.summary: Extended government quarantine, may need to reside in Cat A/B country first

additional_requirements: Pets must arrive as manifested cargo (not cabin or checked baggage on most airlines)
AVS import licence must be presented to authorities on arrival
Pet must be collected from Changi Airport cargo area
Post-arrival vet check may be required

export_licence.name: AVS Export Licence
export_licence.notes: Must be obtained before departure from Singapore

health_certificate.notes: Must meet destination country requirements. Contact destination authority for specific format.

government_endorsement.notes: All export health certificates must be endorsed by AVS

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Rabies-free/low-risk (only CDC Dog Import Form needed)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)

Hong_Kong.notes: Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department (AFCD) regulates pet imports. Hong Kong is rabies-free and maintains this through strict import controls. AFCD portal blocked automated extraction; data compiled from published AFCD guidelines and cross-referenced with established pet transport operator information. Important: Hong Kong is classified separately from mainland China for rabies risk purposes. BNO visa scheme is driving significant HK-to-UK pet transport demand.
Hong_Kong.data_confidence_note: AFCD portal inaccessible during automated extraction. Data compiled from published guidelines and secondary sources. Verify with AFCD before publishing.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Standard ISO microchip. AFCD will scan on arrival.

rabies_vaccination.timing: Not less than 30 days and not more than 12 months before import (for primary vaccination). Booster must be within validity period.
rabies_vaccination.notes: Vaccination window is strict: minimum 30 days before arrival, maximum 12 months.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from Group 2 and Group 3 countries
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from Group 1 countries (rabies-free: UK, Australia, Japan, NZ, etc.)
rabies_titre_test.notes: Hong Kong uses its own country grouping system (Groups 1-3, not to be confused with Australia's groups)

group_1.countries: UK, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, Ireland, Iceland, Hawaii, and other rabies-free territories
group_1.notes: No quarantine for pets from Group 1 rabies-free countries

group_2.countries: USA, Canada, Germany, France, most EU countries, Singapore, and other low-risk countries
group_2.notes: Home confinement typically required for 30 days. With complete titre test and documentation, quarantine may be waived.

group_3.countries: Countries with higher rabies risk or no bilateral agreements
group_3.facility: Government quarantine facility at AFCD Sai Kung Animal Management Centre or equivalent

import_permit.name: Special Permit to Import Animals
import_permit.notes: Must be obtained BEFORE travel. Permit specifies conditions and quarantine requirements.

health_certificate.name: Official veterinary health certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Government veterinarian in country of export
health_certificate.endorsement: Must be endorsed by official veterinary authority of exporting country
health_certificate.notes: Certificate must confirm: vaccination status, microchip number, health status, absence of infectious disease

fighting_dogs: Pit Bull Terrier (including American Pit Bull Terrier)
banned_breeds.status: Fighting dogs are prohibited from import. Existing fighting dogs must be sterilised, microchipped, and kept on leash/muzzle in public.
banned_breeds.known_large_dogs: Large dogs over 20kg may face additional requirements in some housing estates
banned_breeds.notes: Government housing and many private estates have dog breed and size restrictions

additional_requirements: Pets must arrive as manifested cargo (not cabin baggage)
Import permit must be obtained before departure
AFCD inspection on arrival at cargo terminal
Parasite treatment may be required depending on origin country
Dog licence required within 5 days of arrival (dogs over 5 months)

housing_considerations.government_estates: Many public housing estates prohibit dogs entirely or restrict to small breeds
housing_considerations.private_estates: Many private residential developments have pet restrictions or size limits
housing_considerations.notes: Check housing rules before importing a pet. This is a practical concern, not a government import requirement, but affects viability of pet relocation.

export_permit.name: Permission to Export Animals
export_permit.notes: Required for all live animal exports from Hong Kong

health_certificate.issued_by: Government veterinary officer or AFCD-authorised vet

bno_visa_context.description: BNO visa scheme (started Jan 2021) allows Hong Kong BN(O) passport holders and dependents to live and work in UK
bno_visa_context.pet_transport_impact: Significant surge in HK-to-UK pet transport demand since 2021. Ongoing wave of relocations.
bno_visa_context.uk_entry_for_hk_pets: HK is UK-listed country, standard AHC process. No titre test needed for UK entry.
bno_visa_context.notes: This is a commercially significant context. Many BNO families relocating with pets.

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: Separate from mainland China. Classified as rabies-free/low-risk. Only CDC Dog Import Form needed.
classification_by_other_countries.uk_apha: Listed country (standard AHC entry, no titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 4 or 5 (verify current DAFF classification. Titre test, 180-day wait likely, quarantine mandatory)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)

South_Africa.notes: Department of Agriculture, Land Reform and Rural Development (DALRRD) regulates pet imports. South Africa has rabies present in several provinces (KwaZulu-Natal, Eastern Cape, Limpopo particularly). DALRRD portal blocked automated extraction; data compiled from published DALRRD guidelines and cross-referenced with established pet transport operator information. South Africa's outbound classification by other countries is notably affected by its rabies-endemic status.
South_Africa.data_confidence_note: DALRRD portal inaccessible during automated extraction. Data compiled from published guidelines and secondary sources. Verify with DALRRD Directorate of Animal Health before publishing.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before rabies vaccination

rabies_vaccination.timing: Must be administered at least 30 days before import and within 12 months
rabies_vaccination.notes: Vaccination must be documented on health certificate. Booster must be within manufacturer's validity period.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from countries without bilateral veterinary agreements
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from most P1 countries with bilateral agreements (UK, USA, EU, Australia, etc.)
rabies_titre_test.notes: Check current DALRRD requirements as bilateral agreements may affect titre test necessity

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for pets arriving with correct documentation from approved countries. Pets with incomplete documentation may be quarantined at owner's expense.

import_permit.name: Veterinary Import Permit
import_permit.application: Apply through DALRRD (written application or online where available)
import_permit.notes: Must be obtained BEFORE travel. Application requires destination address in South Africa, description of pet, planned port of entry.

health_certificate.name: Official veterinary health certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Government veterinarian in country of export
health_certificate.endorsement: Must be endorsed by official veterinary authority of exporting country
health_certificate.format: DALRRD may have specific certificate format requirements. Check with DALRRD.
health_certificate.notes: Certificate must confirm: vaccination status, microchip, health status, parasite treatment, absence of notifiable diseases

other_vaccinations.notes: Core vaccinations must be current. Some may be required on health certificate.

parasite_treatment.internal: Deworming within 30 days of export (broad-spectrum anthelmintic)
parasite_treatment.external: Tick and flea treatment within 30 days of export
parasite_treatment.notes: Treatment details must be recorded on health certificate. South Africa has significant tick-borne disease risk.

banned_breeds.legislation: No national breed-specific legislation for imports
banned_breeds.municipal_variations: Some municipalities have bylaws restricting certain breeds (Pit Bull types, Rottweiler)
banned_breeds.notes: No federal import breed ban, but check destination municipality bylaws. Less restrictive than UK, Germany, or France for imports.

additional_requirements: Veterinary inspection on arrival by state veterinarian
Import permit must be presented at port of entry
Pets arriving without correct documentation may be returned or quarantined
Some provinces may have additional requirements for specific diseases
Heartworm, biliary (tick fever), and ehrlichiosis are prevalent: prophylactic treatment recommended for pets entering SA

disease_risks_in_country.note: Relevant for pets living in SA and for export health requirements
disease_risks_in_country.rabies: Endemic in several provinces, particularly KwaZulu-Natal, Eastern Cape, Limpopo, Mpumalanga
disease_risks_in_country.notes: These disease risks affect SA's classification by importing countries. Australian requirement for Ehrlichia canis testing originates from this risk.

export_permit.name: Veterinary Export Permit
export_permit.issuing_authority: DALRRD Directorate of Animal Health (via State Veterinarian)
export_permit.notes: Required for all live animal exports. Apply through provincial State Veterinarian office.

health_certificate.timing: Within 10 days of departure (or as specified by destination country)
health_certificate.notes: Must meet destination country requirements. Critical: many countries classify SA as high-risk due to rabies status.

ehrlichia_canis_test.required_by: Australia (mandatory for dogs), potentially other rabies-free destinations
ehrlichia_canis_test.notes: Dogs exported from South Africa to Australia MUST test negative for Ehrlichia canis. Tick-borne disease testing is a common export requirement.

government_endorsement.notes: All export health certificates must be endorsed

classification_by_other_countries.usa_cdc: HIGH-RISK for dog rabies. Foreign-vaccinated dog process applies. CDC-registered facility required. 28-day quarantine possible.
classification_by_other_countries.uk_apha: Listed country (standard AHC entry, no titre test needed for UK entry)
classification_by_other_countries.australia_daff: Group 4 or 5 (titre test, 180-day wait, quarantine mandatory, Ehrlichia canis test for dogs, import permit, 6-month+ prep)
classification_by_other_countries.eu: Listed third country (standard entry, no titre test)
classification_by_other_countries.canada_cfia: May be affected by commercial dog import suspension (verify current CFIA status for personal pets)

uk_to_australia.complexity: High (quarantine mandatory, titre test, import permit)

key_requirements: May need to transit through approved country (e.g. UK)
Complete all AU import requirements from transit country
uae_to_australia.notes: Most complex route in matrix. Professional agent essential.

key_requirements: Rabies vaccination certificate for dogs 3+ months
CDC Dog Import Form for return

hk_to_uk.notes: High-demand route due to BNO visa scheme. HK rabies-free status simplifies UK entry.

hk_to_australia.origin_classification: HK is AU Group 4 or 5 (verify DAFF classification)
hk_to_australia.notes: Second major BNO-adjacent route. Long lead time is the constraint.

sa_to_uk.notes: Despite SA rabies-endemic status, UK entry is standard listed-country process.

sa_to_australia.notes: Ehrlichia canis testing is SA-specific requirement for Australia entry.

sa_to_usa.notes: Same high-risk process as UAE-to-USA. CDC facility reservation critical.

key_requirements: Enter at designated point of entry
uk_to_france.notes: Post-Brexit change: GB pet passport no longer valid. AHC required. Short Channel crossing route popular.

uk_to_singapore.notes: Simplest Asian destination from UK. Category A status means no quarantine.

sg_to_australia.notes: Group 3 advantage: no 180-day wait. Still requires quarantine.

au_to_singapore.notes: Both countries rabies-free. Simplest bilateral route.

key_requirements: EU Pet Passport with current rabies vaccination and microchip
de_to_fr.notes: Simplest possible route. EU pet passport = free movement. Lowest priority for content.

vaccination_timing_matrix.description: Cross-reference of vaccination timing requirements by destination
vaccination_timing_matrix.usa_entry_low_risk: No wait required (vaccination recommended not required)
vaccination_timing_matrix.australia_entry_group3: 30 days between two vaccinations, then titre test
vaccination_timing_matrix.australia_entry_group4: 30 days between vaccinations, titre test, then 180-day wait from blood draw
vaccination_timing_matrix.canada_entry: No specific wait period stated (must have valid certificate)
vaccination_timing_matrix.singapore_entry_cat_b_c: At least 30 days before export, within 12 months
vaccination_timing_matrix.hong_kong_entry: At least 30 days before import, within 12 months
vaccination_timing_matrix.south_africa_entry: At least 30 days before import, within 12 months

microchip_before_vaccination_rule.notes: Universal best practice: ALWAYS microchip before vaccination regardless of destination. Vaccine may be considered invalid otherwise.

breed_restriction_severity.description: Ranking of breed import restrictions by severity across P1 countries
breed_restriction_severity.most_restrictive: France (Category 1 breeds outright banned from import, Category 2 requires permit + sterilisation + insurance)
breed_restriction_severity.very_restrictive: Germany (4 breeds banned from import at federal level, Bundeslaender add more), Singapore (8+ breeds prohibited including Akita)
breed_restriction_severity.restrictive: UK (4 types banned under Dangerous Dogs Act, type-based assessment)
breed_restriction_severity.moderate: Hong Kong (4 fighting dog types prohibited), UAE (varies by emirate, 8+ breeds restricted)
breed_restriction_severity.least_restrictive: Australia (no federal import breed ban, state-level restrictions), Canada (no federal ban, provincial/municipal BSL), USA (no federal BSL), South Africa (no national import breed ban)
